purpose: Merged semantic and matching core for T-mode to reduce project file count.

dual_module_matching_rules.purpose: Two-sided validation for T-mode module assignment: term-side semantics + module-side semantics.

components.siblings: compute as children of the same parent excluding the current term

module_semantic_hints.purpose: Module-side semantic hints for T-mode screening.

ptr_context_rules.purpose: Rules for selecting exactly one active Problem/Target/Result context per T row.

Problem.description: Problem state, deficit, barrier, harmful condition, or obstacle.

Target.description: Behavior or state intended to be changed, increased, maintained, or achieved.

Result.description: Observed effect, evaluation result, measured outcome, or comparative finding.

problem_gate_enhancement.purpose: Increase sensitivity to explicit and implicit problem signals without collapsing target into problem.

direct_problem_detection.rule: direct deficit/harm/obstacle statements may activate Problem

gap_or_deficit_detection.rule: baseline deficits, insufficiency cues, and weak/negative effect cues may activate Problem

target_derived_problem_inference.rule: target may imply hidden deficit only when supported by at least one deficit/insufficiency/rationale cue

pattern_module_matches_schema.purpose: Internal metadata schema backing plus-assignments in T tables with BCIO IDs.

bcio_semantic_link_rules.purpose: Rules for creating candidate semantic links between BCIO entities based on shared semantic keywords.

output_record_schema.source_id: BCIO ID of first entity in canonical pair order
output_record_schema.target_id: BCIO ID of second entity in canonical pair order
output_record_schema.shared_keywords: array of normalized shared semantic keywords after stopword filtering
output_record_schema.module_intersection: shared modules between source and target
output_record_schema.same_ontology: true if source and target have same source_ontology

stage_narrative_ontology.purpose: Hierarchical stage narrative ontology for Problem, Goal, Result, and Context. Used to select a whole-stage narrative root, then refine through children and siblings before module matching.

root_concepts.label: goal
root_concepts.definition: A <cognitive representation> of an end state towards which one is striving.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Core goal narrative concept.

root_concepts.label: goal setting process
root_concepts.definition: A <mental process> that establishes a cognitive representation of the desired end state.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Narrative of setting a goal.

root_concepts.label: goal pursuit process
root_concepts.definition: A <bodily process> in which attempts are made to achieve a desired end state.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Narrative of pursuing a goal.

root_concepts.label: mental plan for a behaviour
root_concepts.definition: A <mental plan> that is realised in mental processes representing the steps to enact a behaviour and the conditions under which the behaviour will be enacted.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Narrative of behavioural planning linked to a goal.

root_concepts.label: goal directed BCT
root_concepts.definition: A <behaviour change technique> that sets or changes goals.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Narrative where the goal appears as a goal-directed intervention technique.

root_concepts.label: belief about barriers to a behaviour
root_concepts.definition: A <belief> about a factor that could restrict or impede a person from engaging in a behaviour.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Problem narrative framed as barrier to behaviour.

root_concepts.label: belief about severity of an outcome
root_concepts.definition: A <belief> about how serious the harm associated with an outcome could be.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Problem narrative framed as severity of an outcome.

root_concepts.label: knowledge regarding a threat
root_concepts.definition: <Knowledge> regarding a potential harm.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Problem narrative framed as threat-related knowledge.

root_concepts.label: healthcare access
root_concepts.definition: A <behavioural opportunity> regarding how easy it is for a person to approach and use a healthcare service.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Problem narrative framed as access problem.

root_concepts.label: mental plan for coping with barriers
root_concepts.definition: A <mental plan for a behaviour> regarding a strategy to deal with conditions that could prevent the performance of a behaviour.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Problem narrative framed as coping with barriers.

root_concepts.label: behaviour change intervention outcome behaviour
root_concepts.definition: Human behaviour that is an intervention outcome.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Result narrative framed as outcome behaviour.

root_concepts.label: belief about gain
root_concepts.definition: A <belief> about the existence of an increase from an initial reference point.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Result narrative framed as gain.

root_concepts.label: belief about reduction
root_concepts.definition: A <belief> about the existence of a decrease from an initial reference point.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Result narrative framed as reduction.

root_concepts.label: tobacco smoking behaviour
root_concepts.definition: A tobacco use behaviour that involves setting light to a combustible tobacco product and ingesting the tobacco smoke that is produced.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Result narrative framed as tobacco smoking behaviour outcome zone.

root_concepts.label: cigarette smoking behaviour
root_concepts.definition: A tobacco smoking behaviour that involves setting light to one end of a cigarette, putting the other end to the lips and sucking in order to ingest cigarette smoke.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Result narrative framed as cigarette smoking behaviour outcome zone.

root_concepts.label: behaviour change intervention context
root_concepts.definition: An aggregate of entities that are not dependent on the intervention but may influence the effect of a BCI on its outcome behaviour.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Context narrative root.

root_concepts.label: location
root_concepts.definition: A spatial <quality> that inheres in a bearer by virtue of its position relative to other entities.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Context narrative framed as location.

root_concepts.label: financial behavioural opportunity
root_concepts.definition: A <physical behavioural opportunity> that involves the person having sufficient financial resources.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Context narrative framed as financial behavioural opportunity.

root_concepts.label: social environmental system
root_concepts.definition: An <environmental system> that consists of people, their pets, social and cultural institutions and processes that influence the life of people and social groups.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Context narrative framed as social environmental system.

root_concepts.label: employed
root_concepts.definition: An employment status realised by an individual being engaged in an activity or service for wages or salary.
root_concepts.stage_fit_reason: Context narrative framed as employed population status.

stage_vector_rules.purpose: Convert active PTRC stage flags into a stage-to-module influence vector for all 13 modules before ontology matching.

Problem.core_interpretation: Interpret the phrase as a problem-state, barrier-state, deficit-state, risk-state, failure-state, or adverse condition before module matching.

Dose.reason: Problems often surface as absence, insufficiency, excess, or mismatch of intervention amount or exposure.

Schedule.reason: Problems often appear through timing failures, delays, absences, or badly aligned temporal conditions.

Engagement.reason: Problems frequently involve dropout, non-participation, incomplete uptake, or weak adherence to active components.

Reach.reason: Problems often involve low coverage, missed exposure, or failure to reach intended recipients.

Fidelity.reason: Problems often arise when delivery is not as intended, inconsistent, or protocol-incongruent.

BCT.reason: A problem statement may imply a missing or misapplied intervention component, but not always directly.

MoA.reason: A problem may reflect failed mechanisms such as low motivation, low self-efficacy, or barrier-driven processes.

Mode of Delivery.reason: Problems can be linked to how content was or was not delivered, but this is not the default interpretation.

Setting.reason: Some problems are setting-bound, but setting is usually contextual rather than the primary problem carrier.

Source.reason: Problems can involve source mismatch or absent deliverer identity, but this is secondary.

Population.reason: Problem-statements may be located in a recipient group, but the group is not always the problem itself.

Behaviour.reason: Problems often concern problematic behaviour, non-performance, or harmful performance.

Style of Delivery.reason: Style may contribute to a problem, but it is rarely the first interpretation of problem narratives.

Goal.core_interpretation: Interpret the phrase as a desired state, intended direction of change, plan target, or goal-oriented intervention frame before module matching.

Dose.reason: Goals can specify intended amount, target level, or desired exposure conditions.

Schedule.reason: Goals often contain planned time horizons, deadlines, future target states, or intended temporal progression.

Engagement.reason: Some goals concern participation, completion, or adherence, though not all goal phrases do.

Reach.reason: Goals may include intended coverage or uptake, but this is not always central.

Fidelity.reason: Some goals specify planned correct delivery or intended implementation quality.

BCT.reason: Goal narratives strongly align with intervention techniques targeting change, planning, or commitment.

MoA.reason: Goals strongly align with motivational, cognitive, and intentional mechanisms.

Mode of Delivery.reason: Delivery mode may support goals, but is not usually the core goal-space.

Setting.reason: Setting may contextualize a goal, but typically does not define the goal itself.

Source.reason: Source may support goal pursuit, but is not usually the main goal signal.

Population.reason: Goals may be framed for a group, but the group is usually not the main target concept.

Behaviour.reason: Goals frequently specify desired behaviour states or desired behaviour change endpoints.

Style of Delivery.reason: Communication style may support goals, but is not usually the first goal interpretation.

Result.core_interpretation: Interpret the phrase as an observed outcome, measured effect, realised change, achieved state, or post-intervention result before module matching.

Dose.reason: Results may reflect exposure level or delivered amount, but dose is often upstream rather than the result itself.

Schedule.reason: Results may be temporally situated or time-dependent, especially in follow-up outcome statements.

Engagement.reason: Results often concern uptake, completion, participation, retention, or adherence outcomes.

Reach.reason: Results often include achieved coverage, exposure rates, recruitment success, or response levels.

Fidelity.reason: Results may be expressed as achieved implementation quality or delivery as intended.

BCT.reason: A result may refer back to intervention techniques, but the technique is usually not the outcome itself.

MoA.reason: Results can include changed beliefs, motivation, self-efficacy, or other mechanism states.

Mode of Delivery.reason: Mode of delivery rarely acts as the outcome meaning, except in design-reporting contexts.

Setting.reason: Setting rarely carries the result meaning directly.

Source.reason: Source is rarely the direct result construct.

Population.reason: Results may be attached to a group, but the group is usually not the result concept itself.

Behaviour.reason: Results strongly align with observed behaviour change, outcome behaviour, abstinence, uptake, or performance.

Style of Delivery.reason: Style is rarely itself the outcome, except in delivery-evaluation contexts.

Context.core_interpretation: Interpret the phrase as contextual framing, condition setting, recipient group, source framing, environmental setting, or delivery condition before module matching.

Dose.reason: Context can include comparator exposure or no-exposure conditions, but dose is not always the main context carrier.

Schedule.reason: Context often includes timing conditions, follow-up windows, or scheduled conditions.

Engagement.reason: Context may include participation conditions, but engagement is not usually the default context meaning.

Reach.reason: Context may frame who was reached or not reached, but reach is often downstream.

Fidelity.reason: Context can include arm-by-design delivery conditions or protocol conditions.

BCT.reason: Context may mention intervention components, but contextual phrases are not always BCT-first.

MoA.reason: Mechanism terms usually are not the first interpretation of context statements.

Mode of Delivery.reason: Context often specifies channel, medium, or contact format.

Setting.reason: Context strongly aligns with setting, location, environment, institution, or site.

Source.reason: Context often specifies who delivers, who provides, or who communicates.

Population.reason: Context strongly aligns with the recipient group, comparator group, subgroup, or population frame.

Behaviour.reason: Behaviour may appear inside context statements, but is not usually the primary contextual carrier.

Style of Delivery.reason: Context can include interpersonal style, framing style, or communication manner.

operational_module_derived_hierarchy.purpose: Author-defined working hierarchy for operational modules. Built for algorithmic decomposition, retrieval, and ranking rather than imported as a canonical ontology tree.

universal_phrase_algorithm_v3.core_formula: ptrc_flags -> whole_stage_narrative -> stage_vector -> semantic_decomposition -> operational_hierarchy_pass -> 13_module_projection -> downstream_candidate_retrieval
universal_phrase_algorithm_v3.V73_expansion_note: If query is affective/abstract/social/polysemous: Block A (Step 3a process types + Step 3b synonyms/antonyms) feeds into semantic_unit_decomposition. P1->P2->P3 search vectors replace or augment standard decomposition.

two_pass_module_scan_policy.reason: module is no longer the primary first-step routing anchor; coordinate matching via module_position descriptions now precedes module narrowing

step_10_patch_note.purpose: Expose exact-vs-near-exact grounded decision path inside semantic matching core.

pattern_star_execution.purpose: Operationalize Pattern* rendering for near-exact grounded patterns.

problem_gate_enhancement.purpose: Increase sensitivity to explicit and implicit problem signals without collapsing target into problem.

direct_problem_detection.rule: direct deficit/harm/obstacle statements may activate Problem

gap_or_deficit_detection.rule: baseline deficits, insufficiency cues, and weak/negative effect cues may activate Problem

target_derived_problem_inference.rule: target may imply hidden deficit only when supported by at least one deficit/insufficiency/rationale cue

cumulative_step_embed_stage_and_operatio.purpose: Embed stage narrative ontology, stage vector rules, and operational derived hierarchy directly into T_SEMANTIC_MATCHING_CORE_with_neighborhoods.json because no new runtime slots are available.

wave_1_cumulative_runtime_alignment.purpose: Split candidate vs final write, enable abstract/summary coding rules, preserve PTRC while requiring valid module ID for visible rows, and keep Pattern ID separate from ontology ID.

wave_2_cumulative_render_alignment.purpose: Keep candidate search, validated row assembly, and visible rendering as separate semantic states.

wave_3_cumulative_semantic_alignment.purpose: Add narrative normalization, anchor traceability semantics, and row split discipline on top of wave 2.

summary_projection_semantic_policy.purpose: Keep semantic candidate exploration separate from canonical M-summary projection.

screening_summary_semantic_alignment.purpose: Semantic alignment between visible screening rows and canonical M-summary rows.

wave7_audit_consistency_layer.purpose: Keep semantic candidate exploration separate from visible counting and summary projection.

regression_semantic_hardening_policy.purpose: Keep semantic matching behavior stable across edge cases and repeated dialogue.

drefs_hierarchy.purpose: Internal hierarchy for each DREFS module. L1=anchor (module activation), L2=mechanism type, L3=specific mechanism. Algorithm traverses top-down (L1 found -> explore L2/L3) or bottom-up (L3 found -> infer module via parent). Confidence: L1=high, L2=medium, L3=low (requires partner term for high confidence).

_meta_drefs_hierarchy: DREFS_HIERARCHY v1.0 merged. Contains L1/L2/L3 internal hierarchy for all 5 DREFS modules (146 terms). Use for: module activation (L1 anchor), mechanism identification (L2), specificity (L3), gap detection by level, top-down and bottom-up traversal.

V73_semantic_expansion_trigger._note: V73: pre-pipeline expansion check. Fires before universal_phrase_algorithm_v3.

trigger_check.rule: evaluate query term before entering standard decomposition
trigger_check.if_affective_or_abstract: activate Block A expansion (V73_semantic_expansion in EXECUTION_CORE)
trigger_check.if_direct_bcio_match_exists: still run expansion — direct match + expansion always better than direct match alone

expansion_output_feeds_into: P1 -> semantic_unit_decomposition (adds decomposition constructs as search units)
P2 -> bcio_semantic_link_rules (synonyms/antonyms as additional seed terms)
P3 -> downstream_candidate_retrieval_and_ranking (one-hop from P1/P2 results via hierarchy)

integration_with_existing_pipeline.13_module_projection: expansion results projected across all 13 modules (T1-T16 via bcio_by_module.json)
integration_with_existing_pipeline.quality_gate: V73 quality gate replaces simple node count — requires >=4 modules >=5 nodes

V73_graph_semantic_integration._note: V73: graph co-activation and affective patterns integrated into semantic pipeline

graph_pre_activation.output: module_search_priority_order — modules with high co-activation searched first

extracts.failure_cluster: breakdown conditions — add to P1 and gap detection
extracts.gap_pattern: missing evidence description — feeds gap block

variability_combinatorics.rule: for each matched BCIO ID: extract parent + children + siblings -> add as P3 candidates
variability_combinatorics.priority: rank by co_activation_count from graph edges

term_combo_pass.rule: check known productive term pairs -> augment candidate set

problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Behaviour: defect in action, outcome dynamics, behavioural result, predictability of outcome
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Population: defect in group composition, demographic structure, subgroup representation
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Setting: defect in context, place, geography, time, environment
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Mode of Delivery: defect in delivery channel, format, medium
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Source: defect in provider, communicator, source actor
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.BCT_Content: defect in active component, technique, intervention content
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Mechanisms of Action: defect in causal psychological, social or biological mechanism
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Dose: defect in amount, intensity, frequency, duration, repetition
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Reach: defect in coverage, access, inclusion, exclusion, representational capture
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Fidelity: defect in delivered as intended, protocol adherence, implementation fidelity
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Engagement: defect in uptake, participation, use, post-contact involvement
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Outcome: defect in measured end result, effect metric, endpoint
problem_to_module_routing_dictionary.Evaluation_Measurement: defect in data quality, bias, missingness, interpolation, restrictive definitions, scale mismatch
